prompt: Which statement about drug withdrawal times in food animals is correct?

options.text: Observe labeled withdrawal periods so residues clear before slaughter/milk use
Withdrawal times can be ignored if the animal looks healthy
Any human drug may be used without restrictions in cattle
Withdrawal applies only to topical shampoos

explanation: FARAD/label guidance protects the food supply; ELDU has extra rules in food animals.

distractorExplanations.c: Human drugs restricted especially in food animals.

prompt: A drug with a narrow therapeutic index means:

options.text: It is always OTC safe at any dose
Toxic dose is close to effective dose—dose carefully and monitor

explanation: Examples in vet med teaching often include digoxin, some chemo agents—precision matters.

prompt: Bioavailability is lower for many oral drugs in ruminants sometimes because:

options.text: IV and oral are always identical
Rumen microbes/pH can degrade or bind drugs

explanation: Species differences drive route/dose selection.

prompt: Controlled substances in veterinary practice require:

options.text: Sharing vials with other clinics informally without logs
Secure storage, logged use, and DEA compliance

explanation: Like human pharmacies, diversion control is mandatory.

prompt: Which route typically provides the most rapid onset?
explanation: IV for emergencies (e.g., anticonvulsants, emergency drugs) when access available.

prompt: NSAID toxicity in dogs commonly involves:

options.text: GI ulceration and renal injury risks
Keratoconjunctivitis sicca only like sulfa exclusivity
Bone marrow suppression only like estrogen exclusivity

explanation: Never stack NSAIDs with steroids casually; ensure hydration/dosing by weight.

prompt: Insulin syringes are typically marked in:
explanation: Match syringe to insulin concentration to avoid fatal dosing errors.

prompt: Cat acetaminophen (paracetamol) exposure is dangerous because cats lack:

options.text: Adequate glucuronidation pathways—toxicity can be fatal

explanation: Acetaminophen causes methemoglobinemia/hepatotoxicity in cats—emergency.

prompt: Therapeutic drug monitoring is most useful when:

options.text: Drugs have narrow indices or variable kinetics (e.g., phenobarbital, digoxin concepts)

explanation: Serum levels guide dose adjustments with clinical signs.

prompt: Compounded medications should be used when:

options.text: To copy approved drugs cheaply for food animals against guidance always
No suitable approved formulation exists and a veterinarian directs legitimate compounding
As unregulated OTC for any species online without VCPR

explanation: VCPR and quality/regulatory limits apply; caution with compounded sterile products.

distractorExplanations.a: Copying approved drugs for cost can be illegal/inappropriate.

prompt: Which abbreviation means twice daily?
explanation: BID = twice daily; SID = once daily; TID = three times daily.

prompt: A 10 kg cat needs 0.2 mg/kg of a drug available as 1 mg/mL. What volume do you draw?
explanation: Dose = 10 kg × 0.2 mg/kg = 2 mg; volume = 2 mg ÷ 1 mg/mL = 2 mL.

prompt: Which statement about metronidazole in small animals is most accurate?

options.text: It is never used for anaerobic or protozoal infections
It is commonly used for anaerobic bacterial and some protozoal infections under veterinary direction
It is a Schedule II opioid
It must be given only by the IO route

explanation: Metronidazole is used for anaerobes and certain protozoa (e.g., Giardia protocols vary).

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: It is never used for anaerobic or protozoal infections
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: It is a Schedule II opioid
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: It must be given only by the IO route

prompt: Acepromazine is classified primarily as a:
explanation: Acepromazine is a phenothiazine used for sedation (with important contraindications).

prompt: When reconstituting a vaccine, the technician should:

options.text: Use any available water bottle from the break room
Use the diluent supplied/specified and follow label handling and timing
Shake violently after freezing the vial solid
Store reconstituted modified-live vaccine for weeks at room temperature

explanation: Vaccines require correct diluent and handling per manufacturer label.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Use any available water bottle from the break room
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Shake violently after freezing the vial solid
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Store reconstituted modified-live vaccine for weeks at room temperature

prompt: Preanesthetic fasting in healthy adult dogs typically aims to:

options.text: Cause dangerous hypoglycemia intentionally as the goal

explanation: Protocols vary by age/species (neonates/hypoglycemia risk); follow DVM orders.

prompt: ASA physical status classification estimates:

options.text: Anesthetic risk based on systemic disease severity

explanation: ASA I–V (+E emergency) guides planning/monitoring intensity.

prompt: Pulse oximetry estimates:

options.text: Hemoglobin oxygen saturation and pulse rate

explanation: SpO2 can read falsely with poor perfusion/pigment/motion; confirm clinically.

prompt: Capnography (ETCO2) helps assess:
explanation: Sudden ETCO2 drop may mean extubation/arrest/disconnect; high ETCO2 hypoventilation.

prompt: Surgical instrument sterilization for critical items requires:

options.text: Cleaning then autoclave/appropriate sterilization with monitoring
Flash without cleaning as routine for implants

explanation: Same Spaulding logic as human SPD; BI monitoring best practice.

distractorExplanations.b: Alcohol disinfects not sterilizes reliably for critical.

prompt: Aseptic skin prep for surgery commonly uses:

options.text: Dirty clippers wiped once across the site as sufficient prep
Chlorhexidine or iodophor scrub patterns outward from incision site
Hydrogen peroxide as the gold-standard residual antiseptic always
No prep if fur looks clean

explanation: Clippers for hair removal; contact time matters; avoid harsh alcohols pooling with lasers/cautery.

prompt: Pain scoring postoperatively should be:

options.text: Ignored because animals hide pain always as a reason to withhold analgesics
Performed with validated scales and treated per veterinarian orders
Based solely on client Facebook posts

explanation: Multimodal analgesia common; reassess after interventions.

distractorExplanations.a: Hiding pain means assess carefully, not withhold.
distractorExplanations.d: Client input helps but staff assess.

prompt: Extubation readiness in dogs often includes:

options.text: Complete absence of any reflexes forever
When the soda lime is purple only
Return of swallowing reflex (species differences apply—cats often earlier)

explanation: Know species differences and brachycephalic risks (delay extubation carefully).

distractorExplanations.c: CO2 absorbent color is circuit maintenance.

prompt: Surgical wound dehiscence risk increases with:

options.text: Infection, tension, poor nutrition, and self-trauma

explanation: E-collars and activity control protect closures.

prompt: Malignant hyperthermia-like crises under anesthesia are rare but demand:

options.text: Discontinue triggers, hyperventilate with O2, cool, and DVM-directed emergency drugs
Cover with heavy blankets to warm further

explanation: Recognize hot/rigid/hypercarbic patients; team response.

prompt: Capnography primarily monitors:

options.text: End-tidal carbon dioxide as a ventilation indicator

explanation: ETCO2 reflects ventilation and can alert to circuit or patient problems.

prompt: A surgical scrub on a patient should progress:

options.text: From dirty periphery toward the incision site
From the proposed incision site outward in concentric fashion
Only with alcohol and never antiseptic
After the drapes are already contaminated

explanation: Aseptic prep moves from clean (incision) to dirty (periphery).

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: From dirty periphery toward the incision site
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only with alcohol and never antiseptic
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: After the drapes are already contaminated

prompt: Which instrument is typically used to hold drapes to skin?
explanation: Backhaus towel clamps secure drapes to the patient.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Rochester-Carmalt forceps for bowel only

prompt: Hypotension under anesthesia is a concern because it can:

options.text: Reduce organ perfusion and increase anesthetic risk

explanation: Low blood pressure threatens tissue perfusion; treat underlying causes promptly.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Eliminate the need for monitoring

prompt: When preparing a cat for feline castration, an important consideration is:

options.text: Adequate analgesia/anesthesia and aseptic technique for the scrotal approach used
Mandatory overnight fasting for 48 hours in neonates only as a universal rule without assessment
Avoiding any monitoring because the procedure is brief

explanation: Even brief surgeries require appropriate anesthesia, analgesia, and asepsis.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: That cats never need analgesia
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Mandatory overnight fasting for 48 hours in neonates only as a universal rule without assessment
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Avoiding any monitoring because the procedure is brief

prompt: PCV/TS (or TP) together help evaluate:

options.text: Red cell mass and plasma protein/hydration status

explanation: High PCV+TS suggests dehydration; high PCV with normal TS may suggest other causes.

prompt: A left shift on CBC refers to:
explanation: Toxic change and left shift guide infection urgency.

prompt: Azotemia with isosthenuria suggests:

options.text: Always prerenal dehydration with maximal concentration
Renal inability to concentrate—renal azotemia more likely than simple prerenal

explanation: Interpret BUN/crea with USG; prerenal often hypersthenuric if kidneys work.

prompt: SNAP/ELISA in-clinic tests require:

options.text: Reading results the next day always for accuracy
Following timing/temperature instructions and reading within the window

explanation: False results from protocol deviations are common.

prompt: Cytology of an ear swab with Malassezia shows:

options.text: Budding yeast (peanut/footprint shapes) with inflammatory debris often

explanation: Common in canine otitis; treat underlying allergies too.

distractorExplanations.b: Bacteria may co-exist but yeast distinctive.

prompt: Radiographic technique: increasing kVp primarily:

options.text: Only changes number of x-ray photons like mAs solely
Increases beam penetrability/contrast scale changes (more penetration)

explanation: mAs ≈ quantity; kVp ≈ quality/penetration; ALARA still applies.

prompt: A blood sample for glucose in a non-separator tube left on clot too long may:

options.text: Show falsely decreased glucose due to cell metabolism
Convert to ketones only in the syringe label

explanation: Use proper tubes/timing; fluoride tubes inhibit glycolysis when delayed.

prompt: USG is measured with a:
explanation: Interpret hydration/renal concentrating ability with chemistries.

prompt: Crossmatching is performed to:

options.text: Replace blood typing in all species always without typing

explanation: Cats have naturally occurring alloantibodies—type before transfusing; crossmatch also important.

prompt: Ketones on urine dipstick in a diabetic animal suggest:

options.text: Possible DKA risk—needs prompt veterinary assessment

explanation: Dipsticks detect acetoacetate more than beta-hydroxybutyrate—clinical correlation needed.

prompt: A left shift on a CBC refers to:

options.text: Increased numbers of immature neutrophils (bands) in circulation
A change in the X-ray technique chart only

explanation: Left shift = increased immature neutrophils, often with inflammation/infection.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: A change in the X-ray technique chart only

prompt: Which tube additive is commonly used for coagulation testing (e.g., PT/PTT)?

options.text: EDTA (purple top) as first choice for coag panels
No additive red top only for every coag test
Heparin green top exclusively for PT

explanation: Citrate tubes are standard for most coagulation assays.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: EDTA (purple top) as first choice for coag panels
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: No additive red top only for every coag test
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Heparin green top exclusively for PT

prompt: Icteric serum appears:
explanation: Icterus = yellow discoloration from bilirubin; lipemia is milky.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Milky white from lipemia only

prompt: A positive catalase test helps differentiate:

options.text: All Gram-negative rods from each other perfectly

explanation: Catalase separates Staph (+) from Strep (−) among Gram-positive cocci.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: All Gram-negative rods from each other perfectly
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Fungal hyphae species without microscopy

prompt: Which statement about radiographic technique is correct?

options.text: Increasing mAs increases radiographic density (blackness) roughly proportionally
kVp has no effect on contrast or penetration
Grids are used to increase scatter reaching the film/detector
Collimation should always be wide open past the patient

explanation: mAs controls quantity of x-rays/density; collimate tightly; grids reduce scatter.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: kVp has no effect on contrast or penetration
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Grids are used to increase scatter reaching the film/detector
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Collimation should always be wide open past the patient

prompt: Proper dog lifting for a mid-sized patient typically uses:

options.text: Support chest and hindquarters close to your body with team help if needed
Lift by the front limbs only

explanation: Protect staff backs and animal joints; use muzzles/sedation when indicated by DVM for fractious pets.

prompt: Feline restraint with a towel ('purrito') helps:

options.text: Limit limb movement while reducing stress for procedures
Allow unprotected face near a biting cat without caution

explanation: Minimize struggle; consider chemical restraint when needed.

prompt: Core canine vaccines commonly include:

options.text: Only Lyme always as the sole core vaccine
Only influenza always as core everywhere
DA2PP (distemper, adenovirus, parvovirus) and rabies (per guidelines/law)

explanation: Lifestyle non-core (Lepto, Bordetella, etc.) based on risk; AAHA guidelines evolve.

prompt: Dental prophylaxis polishing after scaling is done to:

options.text: Smooth enamel micro-etching and slow plaque reattachment

explanation: Probe/chart teeth; dental rads often needed for full assessment.

prompt: Thoracic radiograph inspiration timing aims to:

options.text: Take exposure at peak inspiration for lung detail
Always expose at full expiration only for lungs
Use lowest mA with massive motion blur intentionally

explanation: Sedation/positioning aids quality; collimate and shield when possible.

distractorExplanations.b: Expiration used for some tracheal collapse comparisons but lungs prefer inspiration.

prompt: Neonatal orphan puppies need:

options.text: Free-choice adult dry food day one
Warmth, appropriate milk replacer, and frequent feeding—not cow's milk as ideal

explanation: Stimulate urination/defecation; monitor weight gain; aseptic bottle hygiene.

prompt: Zoonotic concern with pregnant staff and litter boxes involves:

options.text: Canine parvovirus infecting humans commonly as the pregnancy issue
Heartworm microfilariae in humans via litter
Toxoplasma gondii oocysts in cat feces—delegate litter duty when possible
Kennel cough obligate human pregnancy pathogen from boxes

explanation: Toxoplasmosis education and hygiene matter; many infections foodborne too.

distractorExplanations.a: Parvo not a human pregnancy classic from litter.
distractorExplanations.d: Kennel cough not litter Toxoplasma story.

prompt: Ear canal anatomy relevant to cleaning: the vertical canal leads to the:

options.text: Stomach via Eustachian only as cleaning path

explanation: Know tympanic membrane risk—don't soak ruptured ears with inappropriate cleaners.

prompt: Fluid therapy 'maintenance' rates approximate (dogs teaching ballpark):

options.text: About 40–60 mL/kg/day (formula variations exist—follow DVM orders)
No water ever for azotemic patients as policy

explanation: Shock boluses are larger/aliased differently; calculate carefully by weight/species.

prompt: Proper hospital isolation for parvovirus suspects includes:

options.text: Housing next to neonatal puppies without barriers
Dedicated PPE, disinfection with appropriate parvocidal agents, and traffic control
Using quaternary ammonium alone always assuming full parvo kill without checking label

explanation: Non-enveloped viruses need correct disinfectants (e.g., accelerated H2O2/bleach per label).

prompt: Normal canine rectal temperature is approximately:
explanation: Typical dog rectal temps are roughly 100–102.5°F.

prompt: Which parasite is commonly associated with zoonotic visceral larva migrans from dog feces?

options.text: Demodex canis as a common human intestinal worm

explanation: Toxocara canis is a classic cause of visceral larva migrans risk.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Demodex canis as a common human intestinal worm

prompt: When restraining a fractious cat, a preferred approach is to:

options.text: Use scruffing/force without planning and ignore fear signals
Use Fear Free/low-stress methods, towels/muzzles as appropriate, and chemical restraint when needed per DVM
Hold by the tail tip only
Chase the cat around the room to tire it

explanation: Low-stress handling and appropriate restraint/chemical help reduce injury and fear.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Use scruffing/force without planning and ignore fear signals
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Hold by the tail tip only
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Chase the cat around the room to tire it

prompt: Core vaccines for dogs in many US guidelines commonly include protection against:

options.text: Only Bordetella forever as the sole core antigen
Distemper, adenovirus, parvovirus (and rabies per law/epidemiology)
Only Lyme disease in all regions as core

explanation: DA2P (±P) and rabies are commonly core; lifestyle vaccines vary.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Only Bordetella forever as the sole core antigen
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only Lyme disease in all regions as core
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: No viral antigens at all

prompt: Subcutaneous fluids are typically administered in the:

options.text: Loose skin over the scruff/shoulder area (or other approved SQ sites)

explanation: SQ fluids are given into subcutaneous tissue, commonly dorsal shoulder/scruff region.
